title: Hamilton Beach Fresh Grind Electric Coffee Grinder for Beans, Spices and More, Stainless Steel Blades, Removable Chamber, Makes up to 12 Cups, Black
description:
About this item
EASY CLEANUP — DISHWASHER SAFE GRINDING BOWL: The grinding bowl on this coffee bean grinder is designed with durable stainless steel. It's removable for easy filling and pouring and is dishwasher safe..Frequency : 60 Hz
GRIND ENOUGH TO BREW UP TO 12 CUPS OF COFFEE: If you love the delicious taste of coffee made from freshly ground beans, this electric coffee grinder is perfect for the job whether you're grinding coffee for a quick cup or a full pot
SIMPLE & INTUITIVE: The coffee grinder is so easy to use that you'll want to drink flavorful coffee made from freshly ground coffee beans everyday. Just place the lid on the grinder and press the button to grind coffee. Release to stop grinding.
DURABLE STAINLESS STEEL BLADES: With the durable stainless steel blades, this grinder makes easy work of grinding everything from coffee beans to herbs and spices.
REDUCE CLUTTER WITH HIDDEN CORD STORAGE: Hideaway cord storage at the bottom of the grinder stores the cord neatly and out of sight when it's not in use. Quieter than the leading competitor
reviews_5s:
The Hamilton Beach Fresh Grind Electric Coffee Grinder has been a fantastic addition to my kitchen. This little powerhouse consistently delivers perfectly ground coffee, making my morning brew taste fresher and more flavorful than ever.

One of the features I love is how quiet it is compared to other grinders I've used. It’s great for early mornings when I don’t want to wake the whole house. The compact design fits nicely on my countertop without taking up too much space, and it’s easy to store when not in use.

I also appreciate the versatility of this grinder. It works just as well for grinding spices, which has been a game-changer for my cooking. Cleaning is a breeze, thanks to the removable grinding chamber.

Overall, the Hamilton Beach Fresh Grind Electric Coffee Grinder is efficient, easy to use, and reliable. Whether you're grinding coffee beans or spices, it gets the job done beautifully. I highly recommend it to any coffee lover or home cook looking for a dependable grinder. Five stars!After three years of use, I'm finally writing a review.

This is a very simple coffee grinder. It spins at one speed setting, so if you want a course grind, then don't grind longer than 15 seconds. The longer you grind, the finer it gets.

There are two removable parts: 1) the cap/cup (top), and 2) the cup/blade (bottom). This makes cleaning very easy.

Here's how you use the grinder. 1) Plug in the electrical cord. 2) Fill the cup/blade with coffee beans. 3) Place the cap/cup onto the cup/blade. 4) Press the button for at least 15 seconds. 5) Unplug machine by gripping the plug. 6) Turn machine upside down so the coffee grinds fall into the cap/cup. 7) Remove the machine from the cap/cup. 8) Pour out grinds from cap/cup.

I go through these steps because of some of the reviews. Some people say that they make a mess when they remove the cup/blade. I understand why. The cup/blade has fill lines. This implies that the cup/blade is intended to be removed during operations to remove the finished grinds. But no, do not remove the cup/blade with grinds in the cup/blade. Turn the machine upside down so that the grinds fall into the cap/cup. This is because the cap/cup removes easily, and the cup/blade does not remove easily. You will make a mess struggling with the cup/blade while it is full of grinds.

Another review said the cord get damaged at the base when unplugging. This is an example of the need for detailed instructions. What is obvious to some is not always so. When you unplug, grip the plug and pull. Do not only hold the machine and yank the cord out of the electrical outlet. You will damage the connection between the cord and the machine.

This coffee grinder is very simple, with a good design, well built and reasonably priced. Qualities that you want in any tool.I’m glad I purchased this coffee grinder as it’s really easy for me to take apart and reassemble, as well as ease of use. Also like that there is a place for the cord so it isn’t taking up space when not in use. It isn’t too loud and does a good job grinding the coffee.After having the same handheld coffee grinder for 24 years, I was looking to replace it with a moderately priced high quality workhorse and this is the one. Grinds coffee beans quickly and cleans up easily. The design and size is perfect for making a French press. Love this grinder!Fast, quiet and small. Many will say you need to grind two full container batches of beans for 12-cups when the manufacturer recommends one batch. I tested, and ONE batch works for me perfectly.
More ramblings: Apparently I like a lighter brew, which I think is closer to Dunkin or our local Wawa stores. I struggled with the math of how many beans to use to make a 12 cup pot. it gets tricky as the carafe measures in 6ozs, most use 8 ozs for measuring what a coffee cup us when reality is we all use 11 or 15 oz cups. Then some online guidelines use grams and weight, or tablespoons when I have a nice 1/4 cup measuring spoon. I simply wanted a repeated process "to make full pot of coffee how many beans do I grind?" After invoking the soul of Einstein I found that it takes a LOT of coffee to make that full pot based on many online suggestions, effectively two full grind containers. you'll see many reviews here further confirming that "Have to grind twice". I'll say I am using very basic Green Mountain Breakfast Blend beans, same we used in K-cups. And the double batch recipe created a coffee that was black as the darkest night. Darker than anything I had seen in any cafe all over the US. Looked like used motor oil, and tasted VERY strong. Hmmmm. So I'll say that I did this test even tho this grinder's cup has bean fill level markings which clearly indicated 12 cups... as being one batch of grindign, not two.
I also realized my wife and I were drinking weaker coffee anyway with the K-cups. Its the same single k-cup and we are using a 15oz mug, which would be 1/2 the amount of regular grinds. The purist would say my 15oz cup requires TWO k-cups. But again with the beans I tried the recommendations of all the roasters and coffee shops... and I got motor oil looking coffee. So finally I brewed a new pot. 12 cup full pot and used ONE grind from this grinder. And the coffee was great. I dont even need a measuring spoon... just pour into the grinder cup close to the top. Done.

Just like in craft beers there are people who appreciate a high ABV dark stout beer. Me? I am a light lager guy. I am sure there are people who buy beans from all over the world and like a dark dark brew. Me? I'm a light "morning convenience store" coffee drinker

But then I wonder if this (mis)information of using so much coffee beans is put out by the industry to convince the consumer into spending so much more coffee than what the average drinker is used tu?

Long story short to save you the research and math. The experts and the math says for a standard 12-cup pot you need to run TWO full grind containers. The manufacturer says one. Try both and see what you like.

Plus my plan of just one.. 1/2 the cost of beansThis is the perfect coffee grinder. It is a little loud, but works great. It has had no issues in the 5+ years that I have used it. Nothing better than fresh ground coffee in the morning!Our old one finally crapped out with a wisp of smoke coming out the bottom. Bought this one to replace it. We figure that if it didn’t work well, we could always return it, or whenever is died, it wouldn’t be a big deal to get another one. We’ve had this for a number of weeks and it’s used almost every day. Works great, does the job and keeps on grinding.I've only been using my new grinder for a couple of weeks. I grind flax seeds every morning, so I keep the grinder on my kitchen counter; therefore, I appreciate a relatively short cord. I haven't experienced any of the complaints some people have written about. It's not to invalidate their experiences; it's just that my experience has been pleasant. The machine stays clean in crevices, it doesn't blow seeds out while grinding; it's not so noisy that I have to warn people that I'll be grinding. It comes apart very easily for cleaning and goes back together again very easily. To be honest, since I've had the grinder, I haven't think about it much. It's a good grinder at a good price and I'm very happy with it.They say this is good for coffee and for herbs. I only use it for my coffee. I have tried many and of course I always started out with the one that was least expensive. This one is not the least expensive but it is by far the best you can get your coffee beans to a powder if you would want that And it’s quick I’m glad I bought itEver look at some of your appliances and realize you’ve had them for decades and really they should’ve been thrown out a long time ago. I upgraded my coffee grinder and what a difference decades make really like this one has a cup so you can pull out the coffee without having to dump it upside down . And that is a very nice feature if you’re looking to refresh your coffee grinder, I would choose this one. It ticked off all the boxes.!
reviews_1s:
I had this for about six months, used regularly but not every day. Easy to use, easy to clean, fits in well with my daily routine. I make my coffee with an aeropress, and between that, getting the temperature right, and grinding from whole beans, I've been able to make the best cup of coffee I've ever had. Because I use an aeropress, the consistency of the grind isn't that big a deal, I usually just pulse or run this thing until it's all pretty fine.

The problem, though, is that if you store the power cord on this thing every time (and you should, because you shouldn't just leave your kitchen gadgets plugged in all the time), you have to pull the power cord back at a sharp angle to keep it from getting pinched by the cable storage. Over repeated use, that causes a crack in the rubber plastic that protects the wiring. This morning, though the grinder still functioned, the wire popped and sparked the second I plugged it in.

Again, this is after six months of use. Some people don't see it as a big deal to need to replace a cheap kitchen gadget at the six month mark, but for the huge fire and safety risk this is, to me that changes things.

I put in a support request with the manufacturer so I'll update when I hear back, but for the moment I absolutely recommend you look somewhere else if you don't want to die in a house fire after being electrocuted.

UPDATE: Hamilton Beach got back to me, and offered to replace my coffee grinder with a coffee grinder from either Hamilton Beach or Proctor Silex of a price NO HIGHER THAN $15.99.

That's less money than I spent on this item, and the only coffee grinder offered by either manufacturer within that price point is cheaper and worse than what I originally paid for. I told them to keep it because I just want my money back, and they refused and resent the same form letter. I said I'd take the replacement they offered, and they still haven't sent it.

I do not recommend you buy this or any other product from Hamilton Beach. If I could rate it below one star, I would.I am rating this Hamilton Beach coffee grinder as 3 only stars due to a defective product that was sent to me on my 1st order. After removing the grinder from the box, everything felt pretty solid. Then I open the cap and noticed. that the steel blade was very very loose. The blade could move up and down and wiggle a lot. The blade looks to be either riveted on or welded onto the spindle which seems to float up and down. I decided to return it and already purchased a new on which is arriving soon. The removable bowl is a very nice feature at this price. Other verified buyers responded saying that the blade should be firm and to advised to return which I did. Amazon is great at making returns and always provide excellent customer service when questions arise. Keeping my fingers crossed on a better built model to arrive. Only 3 Stars for now!

Update: I received another of the same model as I returned the first one due to a very very loose blade that moves up and down and wiggles about a 1/4 inch in any direction. The new one arrived and the box looks to be re-taped with clear packing tape on a worn smashed cornered box. After opening, everything seems new except for the same exact problem. I received the same batch of loose re-packed returned boxes that had the same problem. If this loose blade is normal for everyone, then I would look elsewhere for another brand. If this loose blade is not normal, I would approve the seller could just write these off as a lost and only sell good working ones. 1 STAR!I use this grinder daily and I actually use the cord storage. If you want this product to last, do not put the cord away every time you are done using it. This is the second grinding product I have purchased from this manufacturer and they both had issues with the power cord separating. No I didn't wind it too tight. I have common sense. To add to this, it is incredibly loud. if you have kids on the same level do not buy this. You will wake the dead. Just another company putting out absolute garbage and stealing your money.The grinder performed great, right up until it stopped working which was six weeks after purchase (with limited use).
Contacted Hamilton Beach as they were prompt to reply and are willing to replace the grinder …But…
I have to pay the shipping of $12.49 which is almost the price of the original purchase.
I’ll need to think about that considering the grinder has a design flaw with the on switch that failed. I may be throwing more good money at a unreliable product.
Hamilton Beach is non-negotiable on the shipping cost.This is a 4 to 8 cup of coffee grinder with a 1/2 cup 4 fluid oz dry coffee capacity which equals 8 tablespoons. There are 1 to 2 tablespoons of beans or grounds to one 6 ounce cup of coffee. So the most it can make is 8 cups of liquid coffee. One 6oz Cup coffee per 1 tablespoon beans times 8 tablespoons capacity. The maker is math challenged. I spent some time double checking this.
Cleaning is nice, the only problem is the small 1/2 cup capacity.Supposedly this is dishwasher safe for the lid and removable grinding chamber. HOWEVER!!! There are multiple crevices where the water gets in the grinding chamber and you CANNOT take it apart enough to reach those areas after the water gets in the cracks. The water just stays in the cracks and can't dry. I removed the screws even and could not get to the water in those cracks.The box was damaged and clearly had been a return. Inside, the top cap was cracked. Thanks Amazon for sending me a returned broken product.

That said, I was desperate and used it anyway. Without it, no covfefe.

It's garbage. First the bean reservoir is too shallow to hold a meaningful amount of beans. I mean, it looks like it should be large enough when you look at it with the cap on top, but when the cap comes off, the reservoir is very shallow.

Second, the grinding is fine, but it is quite coarse. I couldn't figure out if there was a way to adjust that.

I threw the thing away as soon as I ground my bag of beans. It wasn't worth the inconvenience of returning it and I needed my morning covfefe.

Do yourself a favor and purchase something different (or buy pre-ground beans).The grounds get stuck between the grinding cup and the lid so when you open it, the grinds fly everywhere. It’s enormously messy.
Also, if you plan to use this as an herb grinder, in addition to a coffee grinder, don’t waste your time. I attempted grinding, juniper berries in this, and it barely split them. It definitely does not give a consistent grind to everything.Obviously this has (other) great reviews so take this as a grain of salt, but mine since essentially day 1 seemed to have issues with consistent power. I would hold down the button to grind coffee for roughly 10 seconds and it would keeping shutting off and turning on. I thought it was getting jammed, but now I don’t believe that to be the case.

I only bought this because I accidentally didn’t buy pre-ground coffee. I’ve also owned this for a very short period of time and it’s been lightly used. Maybe 2 small bags of coffee have gone through it in total.

So you can imagine my surprise when the button came off today. To add some irony to the story. I now can’t find the button. I literally ground some coffee, opened the lid, it spilled a little, but the lid back on, went over the sink, flipped the whole thing upside down, gave it a few taps, opened it again (the lid doubles as a bowl), dumped the coffee into the machine and BOOM, button gone. Can’t find it anywhere. Not in garbage disposal, not on the floor, not under the toaster, not mixed in with the coffee grounds, just gone. I’m on vacation so I’m peeved, but if it was a regular work week I’d be even more put off by this.

I strongly recommend you buy another grinder. I used to have one that had a built in timer feature that auto shut off after the coffee was ground. They’re all probably roughly the same, but this one is junk, at least the one they mailed me. Save your money.After engaging the grinder for a long time, the beans appear to be ground; but after the pourover, I find that several beans were roughly ground or not at all. It doesn’t grind completely and consistently.
